title: Leptocladodia viperina
taxonomy: Leptocladodia viperina is a species of green algae, specifically classified within the phylum Chlorophyta. The phylum Chlorophyta is a group of green algae that are eukaryotic and photosynthetic, but lack flagella and are generally unicellular or colonial. Leptocladodia viperina is further classified within the class Chlorophyceae, which is a group of green algae that are often found in freshwater environments. The class Chlorophyceae is characterized by the presence of a cell wall and the ability to undergo photosynthesis. Overall, Leptocladodia viperina's classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships and ecological adaptations.
